The Marvelous Miss Mogas

Hi, I'm Miss Mogas. Lots of people call me different names, like gasoline, petrol or simply 'gas'. My real name is Motor Gasoline, so let me stick with Miss Mogas alright...

I'm very unique in the world of oil. Not only am I the rare female (you already met Mr Crude, Cowboy Crude and Grandpa Brent before), but I am also what oil folks call as 'a blend'. Yup, you can't get me straight out of any refining streams directly because I am a wonderful mix of multiple blending components from the refinery and petchem streams.

If you want a higher octane version of me, there will be more reformate. If you want a lower RVP version of me, there will be less naphtha and LPG. Some countries accept a bit of MTBE in me as well (but not in the States or Canada, oh no siree).

Enough with the technicalities. I just want to tell you how marvelous I am. I'm in hot demand, especially in the summer when everyone wants to go travelling in their big SUVs. I'm not afraid of competition (yeah, I know EV is a big thing in China, but at the moment, I'm still in hot demand in the USA, alright...)

But when it's hot, my blend changes too. The specs change with a lower RVP requirement so I don't overheat in a hotter environment. So don't for one second think that you can spike more butane and naphtha into my drinks now.

I can get pretty exotic too. It doesn't mean that I have to be made in the USA to be used in the USA. My posh cousin Miss ARA usually sends a version of me into New York whenever the Transatlantic arbitrage (TA arb) is open. That's when RBOB prices are higher than EBOB, factoring in the TC14 freight cost. 

Most recently in March, I was even hot property in Australia and got shipped there from Houston. Yes, you may get my autograph because I don't come that often, baby.

So what you do think guys? Line up in queue if you think my price tag (which is the RBOB crack) is worth it😉

As companies rely on artificial intelligence to increase capital expenditures, banks and private credit institutions compete for customers, and the commercial loan business shows signs of recovery.

From left to right: Bank of America Chairman and CEO Brian Moynihan, JPMorgan Chase Chairman and CEO Jamie Dimon, and Citigroup CEO Jane Fraser attended the Senate Banking Committee hearing and testified.

The market generally expects that when major banks, led by JPMorgan Chase and Bank of America, release their second-quarter earnings reports on Tuesday, revenue from stock and fixed-income trading businesses will approach or even set a new record high set earlier this year.

Mike Mayo, a senior analyst at Wells Fargo, said that the banking industry is currently in a "golden window period" for industry development: the two profit pillars of investment business representing Wall Street and retail credit for the general real economy have achieved simultaneous growth. Leading U.S.

banks rely on corporate capital market financing business to earn generous fees, and last month's unprecedented large-scale SpaceX IPO was a key boost. At the same time, geopolitical turmoil such as the Iran conflict has intensified the price fluctuations of various assets, and risk trading businesses are also seeing market dividends.

Mayo said in an interview: "This quarter saw the birth of the largest IPO in history, and the total volume of M&A transactions is expected to hit a new annual high.

Stock and fixed-income transactions are simultaneously increasing in many places around the world." The release of this quarter's financial reports from major banks coincides with a rare positive cycle for the industry. In the past few years, the banking industry has been under constant pressure.

It has to deal with the high interest rate environment and is worried about the economic recession caused by inflation. Now there are multiple positive resonances: Wall Street's investment and financing business is booming, the quality of residents' credit assets is stable, and corporate loan demand is picking up for the first time in a long time.

Mayo commented: "It is difficult to encounter a more favorable industry environment than now." He added that, coupled with the Trump administration's policy of deregulating the banking industry, the financial sector has outperformed the market for two consecutive years.

This eye-catching performance has also raised market expectations, and investors will pay close attention to whether the industry's high prosperity can continue until 2027.

JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Citigroup, Wells Fargo, and Goldman Sachs will release their second-quarter results on Tuesday morning, and Morgan Stanley will disclose its financial results on Wednesday.

Core profit engine: substantial increase in revenue from trading and investment banking business Chris McGraty, an analyst at investment bank KBW, estimates that the overall investment banking revenue of the above-mentioned institutions is expected to surge by 26% year-on-year, and the trading business revenue will increase by 14% year-on-year.

The lead underwriters of SpaceX's IPO are Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley. The two investment banks have booked hundreds of millions of dollars in underwriting fees for this listing alone. In addition, the investment banks also undertook the bond issuance business of this newly listed company to obtain service fees.

At the same time, they have the opportunity to undertake the wealth management business of the company's new billionaires and multi-millionaires. Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley also made a "soft dollar" gain from SpaceX's oversubscribed IPO, said Jay Ritter, emeritus professor of finance at the University of Florida's Warrington College of Business.

SpaceX listing ceremony on the Nasdaq trading floor in New York on June 12, 2026. SpaceX CEO Elon Musk spoke remotely on a large screen from the Starbase headquarters in Texas. Ritter explained that the so-called soft dollars are essentially the sharing fees that hedge funds pay investment banks to obtain oversubscribed IPO shares.

"The biggest profit that the IPO business brings to investment banks is not the basic underwriting fee, but the channel of allocating new shares to hedge funds and active mutual funds in exchange for soft dollar shares." McGraty analyzed that the increase in trading business revenue comes from two major drivers: the stock market continued to rise this

quarter, which led to increased stock trading volume; the conflict in Iran pushed up oil prices, interest rates, and exchange rates, and the trading activity in the fixed income market increased significantly.

"Banks are now able to fully capture the benefits of market fluctuations, whereas in previous cycles they often suffered losses due to misjudgments." Real economy: demand for corporate loans picks up But Mayo believes that the more noteworthy benefits this quarter come from the traditional commercial banking sector.

The commercial loan business, which has been sluggish in the past few years, has reached an inflection point: on the one hand, banks and private credit institutions compete for corporate customers; on the other hand, the artificial intelligence industry has spawned a capital expenditure boom across the entire industry chain, and corporate financing needs have fully recovered.

"Enterprises have regarded market uncertainty as the norm and no longer wait and see. They have built new factories, expanded production lines, and promoted investment in various projects. Loan demand has rebounded across the board," Mayo said. This trend is good for regional banks (e.g.

), the proportion of commercial loans in its business structure is much higher than that of large all-purpose banks such as JPMorgan Chase. On May 6, 2026, the construction site of a $16 billion data center built by Related Digital for Oracle and OpenAI in Selling, Michigan.

A large number of local residents oppose the project, worried about the occupation of farmland, tight water and electricity supply and rising costs. Retail banking also remained solid.

In an environment of low unemployment, the overdue rates of residential mortgage loans, car loans, and credit cards have remained low, and losses on credit assets have been controllable.

Risk worries in the private credit market: Although no new large-scale risk events have occurred recently and market concerns have eased, there is still the possibility of thunderstorms in this field.

After the collapse of subprime auto lender Tricolor last year, JPMorgan CEO Dimon warned investors: "Once you find one cockroach, there is often a whole nest hiding behind it." Competition for deposits intensifies: Many banks have to raise deposit interest rates to retain and attract depositors' funds.

In an environment where interest rates are stabilizing or rising, banks' net interest margins will continue to be squeezed. The financial sector has outperformed the market for two consecutive years.

Instead of simply focusing on the outstanding performance of this quarter, investors are more concerned about whether the current boom cycle with multiple positive benefits can be sustained. McGraty said: "We all know that this quarter's results will be very impressive.

The core question that the market is really concerned about is whether the prosperity can be sustained. Can all these dividends be maintained in the long term?"

Warsh’s congressional debut is about to take place, and Wall Street is shouting: You don’t have to give an interest rate path, but please make your views on the economy clear.

Since taking office, Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh has been extremely cautious to avoid signaling the direction of interest rates, which has obscured another piece of information that is crucial to investors, analysts and even other policymakers - how he will respond to economic challenges.

Warsh will appear before Congress on Tuesday and Wednesday, where Fed watchers will be closely watching his views on inflation, the labor market and economic growth, and how those factors interact with interest rates. But if his recent public appearances are any guide, these observers may still find little to gain.

Warsh has pledged to jettison the central bank's so-called "forward guidance" - signals about the path of interest rates. At his first press conference on June 17, he avoided answering many related questions.

For example, when asked how patient policymakers would be in waiting for inflation to come down, he responded: "Your question sounds like you're encouraging me to give forward guidance." This has continued to build dissatisfaction outside the Fed, and one of Warsh's influential colleagues has spoken out publicly about it.

In a speech in Rome last week, Fed Governor Christopher Waller made a point of distinguishing between "providing forward guidance" and "illustrating how the central bank might respond under different economic scenarios." He noted that the latter would help reduce uncertainty faced by markets and households, "making life easier for everyone." Goldman Sachs:

The inflationary impact of the Middle East conflict is weakening, and the Federal Reserve is expected to keep interest rates unchanged during the year Goldman Sachs said that the United States and Iran have recently launched attacks on each other again, and shipping risks in the Strait of Hormuz have increased, but the impact on commodity prices caused by the war has significantly receded.

Oil prices have only rebounded modestly and are still about 30% lower than their highs from the end of April to early May; U.S. gasoline prices fell by 15%, aviation fuel prices fell by 35%, and most Gulf export commodities such as methanol, polyethylene and nitrogen fertilizer are close to pre-war levels.

Although sea and air transportation costs continue to rise, international transportation only accounts for 1% to 2% of the import cost of consumer goods in the United States, and its impact on terminal prices is limited.

Although Gulf crude oil flows have declined, they are still above wartime lows, and global visible oil inventories are not at unusually low levels. The Goldman Sachs model shows that the incremental impact of the conflict on core PCE quarter-on-quarter inflation has peaked in the second quarter, especially from May to June.

If the situation does not escalate, the related pressure will weaken significantly in the third and fourth quarters. Supply shortages and supply chain pressures are far lower than during the epidemic, and current inflation expectations have not been significantly anchored.

The marginal impact of superimposed tariffs and AI demand on monthly inflation has subsided. Goldman Sachs expects core PCE to increase by 0.24% month-on-month in June and then maintain at 0.20% to 0.23%; the year-on-year growth rate in August will fall by 0.2 percentage points to 3.2% due to adjustments to statistical methods.

The bank expects the Federal Reserve to keep interest rates unchanged for the rest of 2026, but the policy room for error is limited.

If the conflict escalates again and pushes oil prices back to US$100 per barrel, core inflation may rise an additional 3 to 4 basis points month-on-month in the coming months and significantly increase the risk of interest rate hikes.

SpaceX Starship conducts its 13th test flight on Thursday and 20 V3 satellites will be deployed for the first time SpaceX previewed the 13th comprehensive flight test of its Starship system on the X platform. The earliest launch time is 6:45 pm Eastern Time on Thursday, with a launch window of 90 minutes.

This mission comes approximately seven weeks after the 12th flight on May 22. This test flight will use the latest Starship equipped with a Raptor 3 engine and a super-heavy V3 launch vehicle. The booster will attempt to ignite and return, then splash down in the Gulf of Mexico, while Starship No. 40 will fly along a suborbital path.

The main objectives of the latest test flight include verification of reliable stage separation, engine performance under various conditions and controlled re-entry (re-entry into the Earth's atmosphere). In addition, an important milestone is that SpaceX will deploy 20 new-generation Starlink V3 satellites for the first time.

The Starlink V3 satellite is equipped with advanced laser links for inter-satellite communications, deployable solar arrays and onboard cameras, six of which will capture images of the starship's heat shield during flight. If the 13th mission is successful, SpaceX may be able to conduct the first orbital launch of the starship on the next flight.

SpaceX is working hard to advance the service of Starship to deploy Starlink V3 satellites and serve as a lunar lander for NASA's Artemis lunar landing program. Musk also said previously that this test flight will accumulate key data for the 14th test flight of the "chopstick-shaped robotic arm sea recovery rocket".

MARKET UPDATE — MONDAY, 13 JULY 2026

Markets are beginning the week with two major threats arriving simultaneously:

🔸 renewed US–Iran fighting is pushing oil and bond yields higher;

🔸 a violent semiconductor sell-off is testing the crowded AI trade.

The Nasdaq is under pressure, volatility is rising and Asian markets have fallen sharply.

But this is not yet broad market panic.

It is a concentrated risk-off move centred on technology, energy inflation and geopolitical uncertainty.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 US–IRAN FIGHTING PUTS HORMUZ BACK AT THE CENTRE

The US and Iran exchanged five major waves of attacks over the weekend and into Monday morning, as their struggle for control of the Strait of Hormuz intensified.

The US said it targeted Iranian:

🔸 air-defence systems;

🔸 coastal radar sites;

🔸 missile and drone capabilities;

🔸 military boats operating around the strait.

Iran responded by claiming attacks on US-linked military facilities in Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman and Jordan.

Tehran says the Strait of Hormuz is closed.

Washington disputes that claim and says escorted vessels are still passing.

The most accurate conclusion is that the strait is severely disrupted, but not completely sealed.

Commercial traffic has slowed dramatically as shipping companies avoid exposing vessels and crews to missiles, drones and attacks at sea.

Simple explanation:

The Strait of Hormuz is like a narrow doorway used by a huge part of the world’s energy supply.

The doorway does not need to be physically locked to cause a crisis.

If ships consider it too dangerous to pass, the practical result can be almost the same.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 OIL RISES AS ENERGY-SUPPLY FEARS RETURN

Brent crude climbed approximately 4% towards $79 a barrel, while US WTI crude rose towards $74.

Oil remains below the extreme levels reached earlier in the war, but the renewed increase matters because markets had started expecting shipping conditions to improve.

Those expectations are now being reversed.

Before the conflict, approximately one-fifth of global oil supplies passed through Hormuz.

That makes disruption around the strait a direct threat to global energy availability, inflation and economic growth.

No LNG carriers were reported to have transited Hormuz since Saturday, while European natural-gas prices also rose.

Europe is particularly exposed because its jet-fuel stocks provide less than one month of demand cover.

Britain, France and Germany could face particular pressure if Middle Eastern supplies are interrupted for a prolonged period.

Simple explanation:

Higher oil does not remain confined to petrol stations.

It raises the cost of flying, transporting goods, manufacturing products and delivering food.

Eventually, those costs can reach consumers through higher prices.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Simple explanation:

China’s factories are still selling substantial quantities of goods abroad.

But Chinese households and private companies remain cautious about spending at home.

That makes economic growth increasingly dependent on exports and government support.

Investors will be watching for signs that Beijing may introduce additional fiscal stimulus later this year.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 THE US LABOUR MARKET LOOKS STRONGER THAN IT FEELS

The unemployment rate remains low, but hiring has slowed sharply.

Economists increasingly describe the current environment as “low-hire, low-fire”:

🔸 companies are not dismissing many existing workers;

🔸 but they are also reluctant to create new positions;

🔸 unemployed people and graduates are struggling to get hired;

🔸 fewer workers are confident enough to leave their jobs.

Simple explanation:

The unemployment rate tells us that relatively few people are being fired.

It does not tell us that finding a new job is easy.

The labour market may be relatively safe for people already employed, but extremely difficult for those trying to enter it.

This matters because even employed consumers may spend less when they fear that replacing a lost job would be difficult.

🟢 WHAT TO WATCH THIS WEEK

💠 Tuesday

🔸 June US consumer inflation

🔸 China trade data

🔸 Fed Chair Kevin Warsh testifies before the House Financial Services Committee

🔸 industrial production

🔸 China’s World AI Conference begins in Shanghai

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 WHAT IT MEANS

Today’s market is sending seven important messages.

1️⃣ Hormuz remains the world’s most important immediate market risk

The crisis matters because it threatens one of the world’s most important energy arteries.

Even partial disruption can lift oil, natural gas, shipping and insurance costs.

2️⃣ Oil has become an inflation trade again

A sustained rise would reduce the Federal Reserve’s freedom to cut rates and could increase the risk of further tightening.

3️⃣ The AI trade is being repriced, not disproved

TSMC’s record revenue shows that demand remains powerful.

SK Hynix’s fall shows what can happen when strong fundamentals meet extreme valuations, leverage and crowded positioning.

4️⃣ The market is showing stress, not panic

The Nasdaq is falling much more than the Dow, while the VIX remains near 16.

Investors are reducing exposure selectively rather than selling every asset indiscriminately.

5️⃣ Long-term bond yields remain a major threat

The 30-year Treasury yield above 5% increases financing costs and places pressure on companies whose valuations depend heavily on future growth.

6️⃣ Inflation and earnings now matter even more

Oil has already revived inflation anxiety.

This week’s CPI, PPI, bank earnings and TSMC outlook will reveal whether economic and corporate fundamentals can withstand higher borrowing costs.

7️⃣ Global growth is becoming increasingly uneven

🔸 The US labour market has low layoffs but weak hiring.

🔸China has resilient exports but weak domestic demand.

🔸AI investment remains strong, while consumers and traditional industries face greater pressure.

This is not a simple strong or weak economy.

It is an increasingly divided one.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🟢 BOTTOM LINE

The market entered Monday facing two very different challenges.

The US–Iran conflict threatens the flow of energy through the global economy.

The semiconductor sell-off challenges the valuations supporting global equity markets.

Oil is rising.

Treasury yields are rising.

The Nasdaq is falling.

Volatility is increasing.

Bitcoin is weakening.

Yet TSMC’s record revenue shows that the underlying AI-investment cycle remains strong.

This is not a straightforward collapse in fundamentals.

It is a market confronting excessive expectations, crowded positioning, geopolitical instability and renewed inflation risk—all at the same time.

The next major direction will probably be determined by four forces:

🔸 whether Hormuz traffic deteriorates or recovers;

🔸 whether inflation surprises higher;

🔸 what Kevin Warsh says about interest rates;

🔸 whether bank and semiconductor earnings justify current valuations.

A sudden escalation in the Middle East has triggered a classic risk-off rotation this morning. On Tuesday, July 14th, at 8:30 AM ET, Wall Street faces a high-volatility collision as marquee June CPI data drops simultaneously with major bank Q2 earnings.

Goldman Sachs is in the crosshairs with a $51.3 billion revenue forecast, where analysts will aggressively parse net interest income guidance to see if current rates have flipped from a tailwind to a burden.

If Tuesday's CPI print comes in hot alongside hawkish net interest income guidance from Goldman Sachs, the 10-year Treasury yield will violently break past 4.65%, instantly forcing the small-cap index to surrender its 3,000 baseline. Consequently, this macro double-whammy will likely trigger a sharp liquidity drain across high-multiple tech, dragging $BTC out of its consolidation pattern and flushing it down to test the $60,500 support level before mid-week.

Top Overnight News

The US and Iran exchanged fresh strikes while issuing conflicting declarations over whether the Strait of Hormuz is open to shipping. Oil up 3% BBG

Over the past several weeks, the investment-grade corporate bond market has struggled to absorb a combined $75 billion of bond issuance from NVDA, SPCX, and AMZN. The distribution of investor views surrounding the path of monetary policy in coming months is wide. Goldman economists’ baseline forecast is that the FOMC will leave the policy rate unchanged this year but they assign a 25% probability to a scenario where the Fed hikes. Market pricing is more hawkish, reflecting a base case of nearly 50 bp of hikes through mid-2027, with uncertainty around that outlook. Option pricing signals greater than a 50% likelihood of hikes, but also substantial probabilities to scenarios where the Fed cuts or remains on hold. Conversations with clients reflect a similarly wide range of expectations. Investors also express an unusually wide distribution of views regarding the implications of any given Fed policy path for equities. Goldman

Goldman upgrades oil prices forecast on higher structural security premium

Goldman Sachs raised its long-dated oil price forecast by $9/b to $76/b, citing a higher structural security premium due to ongoing U.S.-Iran tensions and Strait of Hormuz.

Recent tanker attacks and strikes highlight short-term vulnerability, supporting higher prices. However, the bank sees downside risk from new Middle East pipelines expected to bypass Hormuz, insulating over 45% of Gulf exports by end-2027 and 60%+ by 2028.

Goldman on CPI:

We expect a 0.17% increase in June core CPI (vs. +0.3% consensus), corresponding to a year-over-year rate of +2.76% (vs. +2.9% consensus). We expect a 0.11% decline in headline CPI (vs. -0.1% consensus), reflecting lower energy prices. Our forecast is consistent with a 0.24% increase in core PCE in June, reflecting another large increase in its financial services component.

We highlight four key component-level trends we expect to see in the report:
-we expect weak autos inflation
-we forecast benign readings for the shelter categories
-we expect more moderate increases in the travel services categories 
-we expect downward pressure from potential residual seasonality.

We expect monthly core CPI increases around 0.2% over the next couple of months, reflecting the continued slowdown in the shelter categories and the reversal of upward pressure from higher jet fuel prices and the World Cup on the travel services categories, though risks are tilted to the upside if disruptions to oil markets and associated oil price increases prove more persistent than expected.

Just in: Overseas Research and Selection Daily 0713: Goldman Sachs: The inflationary impact of the Middle East conflict has weakened, and the Federal Reserve is expected to keep interest rates unchanged during the year.

Goldman Sachs: The AI boom may push South Korea's exports to exceed one trillion U.S. dollars, and Korean won stability will become the key to growth spillovers Goldman Sachs’ latest research report states that South Korea is becoming the main beneficiary of the global AI boom.

Driven by the surge in memory chip prices, exports are expected to exceed US$1 trillion in 2026. In the first five months, South Korea's memory chip exports increased by nearly 150% year-on-year, about 80% of which came from price increases; semiconductors contributed three-quarters of the increase in exports as of May.

Rising export prices have significantly improved South Korea's terms of trade, rising 8.6% year-on-year in the first quarter and further rising to about 20% in the second quarter. Superimposed on real GDP growth of 3.8%, South Korea's nominal GDP increased by 17.1% year-on-year in the first quarter, the fastest growth rate in 30 years.

Cross-border experience shows that improvements in trade terms usually promote currency appreciation, lower inflation and capital expenditure growth in manufacturing exporting countries; for every 10% improvement in trade terms, the actual capital expenditures of manufacturing exporting countries usually increase by 0.9%.

However, the South Korean won has weakened recently, bucking the trend, which may weaken the boost of AI boom to domestic demand. Goldman Sachs estimates that a 5% depreciation of the Korean won will push up inflation by about 30 basis points and suppress residents' purchasing power and local corporate profits.

At the same time, the weak Korean won's boost to exports has been reduced due to the expansion of overseas production.

If the South Korean government's "three major super projects" are implemented according to the previous semiconductor industry cluster model, the actual capital expenditure growth rate in the next few years can increase by 2 to 3 percentage points annually, the annual GDP growth rate will increase by 0.4 percentage points, and the scale of related public and private investment may increase to 3 to 4 percentage points of GDP.

Goldman Sachs recommends that South Korea prioritize stabilizing the won in the short term, gradually tighten monetary policy in the medium term, and save and reallocate part of its fiscal earnings through a proposed "future response fund." Goldman Sachs: The inflationary impact of the Middle East conflict is weakening, and the Fed is expected to keep

interest rates unchanged during the year Goldman Sachs said that the United States and Iran have recently launched attacks on each other again, and shipping risks in the Strait of Hormuz have increased, but the impact on commodity prices caused by the war has significantly receded.

Oil prices have only rebounded modestly and are still about 30% lower than their highs from the end of April to early May; U.S. gasoline prices fell by 15%, aviation fuel prices fell by 35%, and most Gulf export commodities such as methanol, polyethylene and nitrogen fertilizer are close to pre-war levels.

Although sea and air transportation costs continue to rise, international transportation only accounts for 1% to 2% of the import cost of consumer goods in the United States, and its impact on terminal prices is limited.

Although Gulf crude oil flows have declined, they are still above wartime lows, and global visible oil inventories are not at unusually low levels. The Goldman Sachs model shows that the incremental impact of the conflict on core PCE quarter-on-quarter inflation has peaked in the second quarter, especially from May to June.

If the situation does not escalate, the related pressure will weaken significantly in the third and fourth quarters. Supply shortages and supply chain pressures are far lower than during the epidemic, and current inflation expectations have not been significantly anchored.

The marginal impact of superimposed tariffs and AI demand on monthly inflation has subsided. Goldman Sachs expects core PCE to increase by 0.24% month-on-month in June and then maintain at 0.20% to 0.23%; the year-on-year growth rate in August will fall by 0.2 percentage points to 3.2% due to adjustments to statistical methods.

The bank expects the Federal Reserve to keep interest rates unchanged for the rest of 2026, but the policy room for error is limited.

If the conflict escalates again and pushes oil prices back to US$100 per barrel, core inflation may rise an additional 3 to 4 basis points month-on-month in the coming months and significantly increase the risk of interest rate hikes.

Goldman Sachs: Middle East countries are accelerating the expansion of pipelines bypassing Hormuz, which may cover more than 60% of Gulf oil exports Goldman Sachs said that attacks on oil tankers and the resumption of military operations by the United States and Iran have driven up the recent rise in oil prices, indicating that the Strait of Hormuz remains the most critical source of risk for short-term oil prices.

If the conflict seriously escalates, uncertainty about Gulf exports will re-amplify the risk of upward oil prices. But in the long term, Middle East oil-producing countries are accelerating the construction and restoration of oil pipelines that bypass the Strait of Hormuz.

Goldman Sachs looked at 9 major pipelines and found that the median construction time of historical projects was 2.5 years; supply disruptions usually speed up project progress, but projects involving multi-national coordination take longer. Goldman Sachs evaluated seven pipeline projects that are in the construction, planning or potential expansion stages.

Under the baseline scenario, the effective oil transportation capacity bypassing Hormuz will increase by 3.8 million barrels per day by the end of 2027, with a cumulative increase of 7.3 million barrels per day by the end of 2028, with a total scale of more than 14 million barrels per day, which can guarantee more than 60% of the pre-war 23 million barrels per day export volume of Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, Iraq, Qatar, Bahrain, the United Arab Emirates and Iran.

The total project cost is expected to be US$30 billion to US$48 billion. By the end of 2027, the proportion of protected exports can exceed 45%. If construction speeds up, the coverage ratio will reach 75% by the end of 2028; under a conservative scenario, it will be slightly higher than 45%.

Goldman Sachs previously raised its three-year Brent crude oil price assumption by US$9 to US$76 per barrel, reflecting a higher structural safety premium, but the bypass pipeline expansion may pose a downside risk to long-term oil prices.

Bank of America: Global cloud capital expenditure will reach US$1.15 trillion in 2027, and the AI investment cycle remains sustainable The latest research report from Bank of America Securities predicts that the capital expenditures of the world's largest cloud vendors will reach US$197 billion in the second quarter, a year-on-year increase of 30% and a

year-on-year increase of 83%; capital expenditures in 2026 and 2027 will increase to US$851 billion and US$1.15 trillion respectively, a year-on-year increase of 78% and 35%, significantly higher than the May forecast. Bank of America believes that investment in AI infrastructure is sustainable.

The remaining performance obligations and order backlog disclosed by Microsoft, Amazon, Google and Oracle total more than $2 trillion, providing contract demand support for construction from 2026 to 2028.

At the same time, AI commercialization is accelerating: OpenAI's revenue is expected to increase from US$13 billion in 2025 to US$62 billion in 2027, and Anthropic's annual revenue has exceeded US$47 billion as of May 2026; the number of tokens generated by the agent workload can reach 10 to 1,000 times that of ordinary conversations, further increasing the demand for computing power.

High investment will still squeeze cash flow. Bank of America predicts that cloud vendor capital expenditures will reach approximately 100% to 115% of operating cash flow from 2026 to 2028, and industry free cash flow profit margins will drop to -1%, -5%, and -4% respectively, far lower than the historical 15% to 20%.

However, related expenses can basically be covered by operating cash flow. Since 2026, the five major cloud vendors have raised approximately US$244 billion, mainly long-term debt and permanent equity from 3 to 40 years. Bank of America believes that this is a balance sheet optimization and not a signal of financial pressure.

The bank is optimistic about Nvidia, Broadcom, Marvell, AMD, Micron and the semiconductor equipment industry chain.

Goldman Sachs: AI supply chain accelerates production expansion in Southeast Asia, China’s manufacturing efficiency advantage is still difficult to replace After visiting 10 PCB, network equipment and AI server supply chain companies in Thailand and Vietnam, Goldman Sachs said that relevant manufacturers are expanding production capacity, and the new

projects are mainly for AI servers, CPO switches, optical modules, AI PCB and edge AI equipment, reflecting the continued strong demand for AI infrastructure and the diversification of production bases driven by customers.

Southeast Asia is forming industrial clusters divided by customer ecology: Vietnam mainly undertakes the supply chains of Microsoft, Dell and Apple, Thailand focuses on Google and HP, and Malaysia gathers Amazon suppliers. However, mainland China remains a manufacturing center with its complete supply chain, mature experience and production efficiency.

Labor costs in Thailand and Vietnam are low, but efficiency is also low. The output of a single Chinese worker can be equivalent to up to three Thai workers. If raw materials such as copper-clad laminates are imported from China, tariffs and transportation may push up costs by 15% to 20%.

However, in order to spread geographical risks, customers are willing to accept a cost premium of up to about 20%, and manufacturers are improving efficiency through automation, generative AI and digitalization.

Supported by customer prepayments, equipment subsidies, and demand from cloud vendors, model developers, sovereign AI, and enterprises, Goldman Sachs continues to be optimistic about the AI capital expenditure cycle and has raised its global AI server chip shipment forecasts from 2026 to 2028 by 14%, 22%, and 14% respectively. U.S.

cloud vendors have raised their capital expenditure forecasts by 8%, 27%, and 25%, and Chinese cloud vendors have raised their forecasts by 37%, 44%, and 55%.

Jefferies: Research and development of weight-loss drugs has shifted to muscle-protection and new long-acting drug delivery mechanisms have become the focus of competition Jefferies' latest research report stated that obesity drug research and development is shifting from simply pursuing higher weight loss to improving the quality of weight loss, durability of efficacy and convenience of medication.

Differentiated oral platforms, ultra-long-acting preparations and non-incretin mechanisms have become the focus of capital and industrial layout. ALN1003 developed by Alaunos Therapeutics is an oral non-incretin small molecule. Animal data further shows that it may improve liver indicators and insulin resistance.

However, the company only has about US$350,000 in cash, and financing is the key to advancing clinical applications.

The ASC36 and ASC36_35 compound preparations developed by Ascletis Pharma have submitted clinical applications to the US FDA, targeting monthly or quarterly administration, and monthly combined administration respectively; the half-life of ASC36 in animal testing is approximately 6 times that of similar drugs.

SKNY-1, developed by MIRA Pharmaceuticals, has pharmacokinetic data supporting once-daily oral administration and targets simultaneous appetite, reward and metabolic pathways.

The anti-Activin E antibody "610" developed by the American biotechnology company iBio was combined with semaglutide to reduce lean body mass loss by 73%; RES-010 animal data showed that fat was reduced by about 15%, lean body mass was only reduced by 1%, and the semaglutide group decreased by 8%, and there was no obvious rebound after drug withdrawal.

Jefferies: The U.S. packaged food industry is under pressure due to consumer dissatisfaction, regulatory risks and rising raw material prices. Jefferies' latest research report states that the U.S.

packaged food industry is facing triple pressure: consumer dissatisfaction with food prices is increasing, health and reputation risks caused by ultra-processed foods are rising, and the El Niño phenomenon is pushing up the cost of raw materials such as cocoa and coffee again.

A survey covering 1,100 registered voters showed that 67% of respondents were under significant cost of living pressure, 63% listed groceries as their biggest source of stress, and 67% believed food prices were "unfair." 48% attributed the price increase to tariffs and trade restrictions, 46% believed that companies raised prices to increase profits; 82% believed that the political sector could reduce costs by taking priority actions.

Majorities of Democratic, Republican and independent voters support the government's crackdown on price gouging, even as it increases regulations and puts packaged food companies' pricing strategies under greater political scrutiny. In terms of health risks, the U.S.

Federal Court dismissed an ultra-processed food lawsuit against 11 companies including Kraft Heinz, Mondelēz, Post, General Mills and Nestlé on June 30, on the grounds that the plaintiff failed to prove that the specific company or product had a causal relationship with type 2 diabetes and fatty liver disease.

However, the court acknowledged that the case involved children's health issues, indicating that consumers' concerns about food ingredients are still rising, and the reputational risks of the industry are difficult to subside. On the cost side, New York cocoa futures have risen to about $6,000 per ton.

Jefferies pointed out that heavy rainfall in West Africa and the confirmed El Niño have increased the risk of cocoa diseases and production reductions, and coffee price expectations have also been raised. Its commodity index predicts that the input cost of packaged food will increase by 3% year-on-year in the second half of 2026.

Goldman Sachs first commented on Wisdom and assigned a "neutral" rating with a target price of HK$1,880.

Goldman Sachs covered Knowledge Atlas Technology, the parent company of Zhipu AI, for the first time, giving it a "neutral" rating, with a 12-month target price of HK$1,880, corresponding to a valuation of US$110 billion; the optimistic and pessimistic scenario valuations are US$200 billion and US$54 billion respectively.

Goldman Sachs believes that Zhipu is one of the most competitive manufacturers in China's enterprise-level and programming large model fields. The flagship model GLM-5.2 is close to the global cutting-edge level, ranking second and third in the world in Code Arena and real task agent benchmarks respectively.

A large number of real programming tasks form a data feedback loop, which is expected to support the company in maintaining an iteration speed of one small version every 1 to 2 months and one major version every 7 to 8 months.

Goldman Sachs predicts that the company’s API business’ annualized recurring revenue will reach US$1.5 billion by the end of 2026, which is higher than the company’s US$1 billion target; due to computing power constraints, potential demand is about 2 to 3 times the current supply.

Revenues from 2026 to 2028 are expected to be 6.3 billion yuan, 23.7 billion yuan and 58.8 billion yuan respectively, but there will still be a net loss of approximately 1.3 billion yuan in 2028.

Since the company's listing, the company's stock price has increased by approximately 1,600% (as of the release of the report), the effective circulating share ratio is only approximately 5%, and the current valuation is equivalent to 80 times the expected ARR by the end of 2026.

Goldman Sachs believes that the valuation has basically reflected the leading premium; in the long term, if it shifts from completely open source to an open weight model with commercial terms, revenue and profit margins are expected to be further released.

Major risks include industry competition, high R&D investment, continued losses and Sino-US technological friction.

Goldman Sachs: AI demand drives up ABF substrate gap, market size may reach US$33 billion in 2028 The latest research report from Goldman Sachs stated that the demand for AI servers and general server CPUs is growing faster than expected, and ABF carrier boards may become a new bottleneck in the AI and server industry chain.

The bank has significantly raised its forecast for the compound growth rate of the ABF carrier board market from 33% to 62% from 2025 to 2028, and the market size is expected to increase from US$7.7 billion in 2025 to US$33 billion in 2028. The tight supply and demand has been advanced to the second quarter of 2026.

Goldman Sachs predicts that the ABF carrier board shortage rate will expand from 8% in 2026 to 34% in 2027 and 51% in 2028; if calculated in the second half of the year, the gap in the second half of 2026 will be approximately 14%. The current delivery cycle has been extended from 3 to 4 months at the beginning of the year to more than 12 months.

The industry may continue to be at full production until 2028, and most of the new production capacity will not be released until the second half of 2028. On the demand side, the proportion of AI servers and general servers in total ABF demand is expected to increase from 25% in 2025 to 62% in 2028.

Among them, the compound growth rate of AI server-related demand from 2025 to 2028 is expected to reach 101%, and the proportion will rise to 45% in 2028; the demand for general server CPUs is driven by intelligent AI, and the compound growth rate is expected to be 47%, and the proportion will rise to 17%.

Specification upgrades also increase the carrier board consumption of a single chip. The ABF area required by Nvidia's Rubin GPU is expected to increase by 123% compared to Blackwell, and the Vera CPU requires an increase of 47% compared to Grace.

Goldman Sachs predicts that from the third quarter of 2026 to at least the end of 2027, long-term agreement prices and spot prices will increase by 10% to 15% and more than 20% each quarter respectively, and spot prices may increase by 60% to 80% year-on-year in 2027 and 2028.

The bank expects the second-quarter profits of four carrier board factories in Taiwan to be 4% to 38% higher than market expectations, and raised the target prices of Nanya Circuit Board, Jingshuo, Xinxing and Zhending to NT$2310, 1120, 1140 and NT$824 respectively, maintaining a "buy" rating for the remaining three companies except Xinxing. “存储双雄”又暴跌！📉韩国央行竟然这么说

一、核心政策表态：韩国央行明确周期未到顶
韩国央行周一发布报告反驳市场 “芯片周期见顶” 悲观预期，核心观点：
当前全球半导体供需格局依旧供不应求，AI 基建持续拉动存储需求，但高端 HBM 产能扩产受工艺、良率约束，供给释放速度远慢于需求；
本轮周期和传统存储复苏完全不同，底层逻辑是 AI 全面渗透带来产业变革，云厂商持续资本开支具备长期持续性；
HBM 等定制化高端存储生产壁垒高、扩产周期长，行业高景气扩张将维持较长时间；
多家海外大行（大摩、小摩、高盛）一致预判，至少 2027 年前半导体需求保持强劲。

二、盘面反差：利好发声当日，存储龙头大幅杀跌
消息落地当天韩股走弱，存储双雄同步暴跌：
SK 海力士大跌近 8%，三星电子跌近 4%；
近期调整幅度显著：7 月 2 日三星大跌 9%、SK 海力士跌近 15%；即便三星公布二季度近 599 亿美元超预期利润，股价依旧高开低走大跌 7%。

三、股价下跌四大核心矛盾
1. 资金提前博弈远期供给过剩（核心分歧）
短期 HBM 订单锁满、现货紧缺是共识，但市场担忧三星、SK 海力士持续加码先进产线，2027-2028 年集中扩产落地后，存储供需反转；周期板块资金习惯提前计价远期利空，高位估值先行消化。

2. 高位获利盘集中兑现
上半年 HBM 行情驱动存储股大幅上涨，7 月进入机构半年净值结算窗口，海外公募、对冲基金集中止盈减仓，抛压集中释放。

3. 基本面结构性割裂，估值存在隐忧
景气高度集中于 AI 服务器 HBM，手机、PC 等消费端存储需求持续疲软；市场担忧 AI 资本开支依赖举债，行业估值高位、一旦云厂商收缩 capex，存储需求弹性快速回落。

4. 中东地缘风险压制风险偏好
霍尔木兹海峡通行受限，双重冲击韩系存储产业链：
1）韩国 80% 原油、大量 LNG 经海峡进口，能源涨价抬升晶圆厂电力成本；
2）卡塔尔供给全球近 30% 电子级氦气，氦气是光刻、刻蚀必备原料，物流受阻带来特种气体涨价、供应短缺隐忧，压制芯片板块风险偏好。

四、行情核心矛盾总结
          中长期产业逻辑（央行 / 机构）：AI 算力持续扩容，HBM 工艺壁垒高、扩产受限，存储超级周期延续；短期订单饱满、长协锁定业绩，基本面韧性充足。
         短期交易逻辑（二级市场）：股价上半年涨幅巨大，资金提前博弈远期产能过剩 + 半年报止盈离场 + 中东地缘避险，引发估值回调。
         结构性机会：行情分化加剧，仅高端 HBM、存储设备、国产存储材料具备持续逻辑，普通消费类存储承压。

五、后续跟踪关键观察点
HBM4 价格走势、大厂长期供货协议签署情况；
三星、SK 海力士新增产能爬坡进度，供给释放节奏；
云厂商 AI 资本开支指引是否下调；
霍尔木兹海峡通航、氦气等电子特气供应链变化；
国内长鑫科技上市带来存储板块估值重塑。

SK 海力士大跌近 8%，三星电子跌近 4%；
近期调整幅度显著：7 月 2 日三星大跌 9%、SK 海力士跌近 15%；即便三星公布二季度近 599 亿美元超预期利润，股价依旧高开低走大跌 7%。

三、股价下跌四大核心矛盾
1. 资金提前博弈远期供给过剩（核心分歧）
短期 HBM 订单锁满、现货紧缺是共识，但市场担忧三星、SK 海力士持续加码先进产线，2027-2028 年集中扩产落地后，存储供需反转；周期板块资金习惯提前计价远期利空，高位估值先行消化。

2. 高位获利盘集中兑现
上半年 HBM 行情驱动存储股大幅上涨，7 月进入机构半年净值结算窗口，海外公募、对冲基金集中止盈减仓，抛压集中释放。

3. 基本面结构性割裂，估值存在隐忧
景气高度集中于 AI 服务器 HBM，手机、PC 等消费端存储需求持续疲软；市场担忧 AI 资本开支依赖举债，行业估值高位、一旦云厂商收缩 capex，存储需求弹性快速回落。

4. 中东地缘风险压制风险偏好
霍尔木兹海峡通行受限，双重冲击韩系存储产业链：
1）韩国 80% 原油、大量 LNG 经海峡进口，能源涨价抬升晶圆厂电力成本；
2）卡塔尔供给全球近 30% 电子级氦气，氦气是光刻、刻蚀必备原料，物流受阻带来特种气体涨价、供应短缺隐忧，压制芯片板块风险偏好。

四、行情核心矛盾总结
          中长期产业逻辑（央行 / 机构）：AI 算力持续扩容，HBM 工艺壁垒高、扩产受限，存储超级周期延续；短期订单饱满、长协锁定业绩，基本面韧性充足。
         短期交易逻辑（二级市场）：股价上半年涨幅巨大，资金提前博弈远期产能过剩 + 半年报止盈离场 + 中东地缘避险，引发估值回调。
         结构性机会：行情分化加剧，仅高端 HBM、存储设备、国产存储材料具备持续逻辑，普通消费类存储承压。

五、后续跟踪关键观察点
HBM4 价格走势、大厂长期供货协议签署情况；
三星、SK 海力士新增产能爬坡进度，供给释放节奏；
云厂商 AI 资本开支指引是否下调；
霍尔木兹海峡通航、氦气等电子特气供应链变化；
国内长鑫科技上市带来存储板块估值重塑。

战争在打，能定价的市场却没开门 —— CME 重开前的最后 3 小时（07-12）

⭐ 一句话：过去三个小时中东连续升级，而 $BTC 一动没动 —— 34 美元。今早报 $64,135，现在 $64,169。

期间发生了什么：伊朗革命卫队正式宣布关闭霍尔木兹海峡、伊朗导弹/无人机打了六个国家的美军基地、科威特一座海上石油钻井平台被炸、美军刚刚打掉霍尔木兹周边的反舰导弹与防空系统。

别把这个「没反应」读成「市场认为无所谓」。真相是：能给这些消息定价的市场（原油、黄金、美股期货）现在根本没开门，而 crypto 是 0.35 倍地量的周末薄盘。定价不会不发生，只是被推迟到 —— 今天 15:00（加州时间）CME 重开，距现在不到 3 小时。

🌍 一、宏观：三天的战争，一分钱都还没定价
美股、原油、黄金全部休市，第二天了。屏幕上挂着的还是周五收盘锚：纳指 26,281.61（+0.29%）｜标普 7,575.39（+0.42%）｜道指 52,637.01（+0.29%）｜费半 12,967.16（+0.06%）｜NVDA $210.96（+4.03%）领涨｜VIX 15.03｜WTI 原油 $71.41｜黄金 $4,113.70。

这一整排数字全部是【战前定价】—— 周五收盘时，海峡还没「关」、六国美军基地还没挨打、科威特油井还没被炸。它们已经过期了。今天 15:00 CME 重开 = 油/金/美股期货一次性补定价三天地缘，这是本周第一个、也是最大的跳空窗口。

🛢️ 二、国际形势：「关了」和「没关」同时是真的
升级是真的：美军一周内第三轮空袭伊朗（约 140 个目标），阿巴斯港、格什姆岛均遭袭；伊朗弹道导弹反击卡塔尔、阿曼、巴林、科威特、约旦、阿联酋六国境内美军目标；伊朗波斯湾海峡管理局称海峡「目前无法通行」，革命卫队正式宣布关闭。

但美国中央司令部、特朗普、联合海上信息中心三方同时否认：海峡是国际水道、南部（阿曼一侧）航道双向通行正常、美军护航就位。

谁在说真话？看船，不看嘴 —— 过去 24 小时仅 11 艘商船通过（8 油轮 + 3 货船），正常日均约 34 艘、峰值 59 艘。独立研究机构 HFI Research 最新观察也对得上：伊朗一侧航道仍有船只，但整体航运十分冷清。

👉 结论：这不是「封锁」，是【通行量砍掉三分之二的争议性半关闭】。差别很大 —— 完全封锁油价是跳空级，打折封锁只是溢价级。

❗ 但今天有两处质变：
1）科威特国家石油公司一座海上钻井平台被无人机炸伤 —— 此前挨打的都是船只和军事目标，这是第一个被直接击中的能源生产设施。供给端的性质从「运输受阻」变成了「产能受损」。
2）美军成体系打击了霍尔木兹周边的反舰导弹阵地、防空系统、革命卫队快艇 —— 问题从「伊朗想不想关海峡」变成了「伊朗还有没有能力关」。方向是拧着的：打击进行中 = 短期利多油价；封锁能力被逐步摧毁 = 中期把溢价往回压。这两股力量会在 15:00 CME 开盘时正面打架，别想当然认定只有一个方向。

⚠️ 还有一处要升级理解：伊朗议员今日明说「今后霍尔木兹海峡的管理将由伊朗方面安排，这是伊朗的【最终】政策」。伊朗要的不是一次临时封锁，是改掉海峡的【管理制度】。如果这成真，油价里要长期含一块结构性溢价，而不是打一波就回落的脉冲。

同时谈判在加速：阿曼「双航道」方案（南航道自由通行 / 北航道需伊朗批准、不收费）；伊阿同意继续技术+政治谈判、卡塔尔参与；伊拉克总理周一访华盛顿签油气协议 + 重建绕开霍尔木兹的输油管（需 2-3 年）。

📊 三、技术面（强弱：ETH ＞ BTC ＞ SOL）
🔵 BTC $64,169（24h -0.17%）｜区间 63,605–64,439
　1H 站上 MA20/MA50（64,010 / 64,081），1H MACD 柱转正（+47）= 极微弱的企稳
　但 4H MACD 柱仍为负（-64），动能没回来；日线 RSI 53.5、MACD 零轴下方金叉
　🔴 分水岭：日线 MA50 = 64,670（昨晚 64,944、前天 65,136 —— 均线在往下走，压顶线自己在下移来找价格）

🟢 ETH $1,820（-0.16%，仍最强）｜区间 1,778–1,829.5
　稳稳站在日线 MA50（1,758）上方｜日线 RSI 58.6、MACD 柱 +43｜4H/1H 全在均线上方

🔴 SOL $77.53（-0.64%，最弱）｜区间 75.58–78.30
　4H 跌破 MA20（77.85）和 MA50（79.25）= 空头排列，RSI 45.5

⭐ 成交量才是今天最要命的一格：BTC 最近一根完整日线成交量只有 20 日均量的 0.35 倍；盘中 1H 量比 BTC 0.45、ETH 0.59、SOL 0.42 —— 地量在加深，不是在恢复。
🧭 换句话说：不是「BTC 冷静地消化了战争」，是【房间里根本没人】。周末薄盘里的价格不是信息，这三个小时的「稳」不能当成「市场消化了战争」来读。

⚙️ 四、衍生品结构
资金费率（8小时）：BTC +0.0088% / +0.0061%（温和偏多）｜ETH 【-0.0037%】（比今早更负）/ +0.0020%｜SOL +0.0050%
未平仓合约（OI）24h 几乎没动：BTC $471.5 亿（+0.19%）｜ETH $251.9 亿（+0.03%）｜SOL $50.5 亿（-0.62%，唯一减仓）
24h 爆仓：BTC $1,780 万（多头 1.89 : 1 被打）｜ETH $3,434 万（多 1.18 : 1）｜SOL $1,105 万（多头 10 : 1 被血洗）

⭐ 本段最重要的变化：ETH 那台「轧空发动机」的燃料基本烧完了 —— 前天空头还被轧 4.8:1，今天已经反过来是多头在挨打。ETH 还是最强的，但托着它上涨的强制平仓买盘正在消失。SOL 是反面：多头在被单边收割。

🎯 五、BTC 核心指标
🔴 现货溢价：-$41.4 / -0.0645%（今早 -$39.5）—— 不但没转正，还略微走阔。美国机构现货买盘持续缺席，这条线一天不转正，反弹就一天缺主力。
🔴 恐惧贪婪指数：26（恐惧），与今早持平
🔵 BTC DVOL（隐含波动率）36.44 ｜ ETH DVOL 51.08
⭐ 隐含 vs 已实现（纠正一个想当然）：BTC 隐含 36.3 vs 【7 日已实现 34.9】= 只贵了 1.4 个点，波动率并不便宜；只有跟 30 日已实现 42.1 比才显得低。
　👉 所以别用「波动率白菜价」当买期权的理由。真正的理由是【窗口里塞着两颗有日期的炸弹】（今天 CME 重开、周二 CPI），而隐含波动率是按过去两周的平静定价的。
🔵 MaxPain：7/13 = 63,500（PCR 2.03，put 明显偏重）｜7/14 = 63,500｜7/15-16 = 64,000

🧭 六、综合判断 + 交易建议
核心矛盾：一场正在打的战争 vs 一组还没定价的市场 —— 而 crypto 在一个空房间里独自报价。
⏳ 核心纪律：距 15:00 CME 重开不到 3 小时。【地量 + 一个已知的跳空窗口 = 现在开单边是赌头条，不是交易】。想表达波动就用跨式（7/15–7/17 到期），不赌方向，也不怕「打谈交替」来回反转。

👉 BTC：分水岭仍是日线 MA50 【64,670】
　· 破 63,600 才做空（止损 64,700，目标 62,400 → 61,500）
　· 放量站上 64,670 且现货溢价转正 → 空头框架作废，看 68,000–70,000；溢价不转正就当假突破
　· 没破位不空，没站上不追。中间这段就是不该有仓位的地方。

👉 ETH：最强、站稳 1,758 偏多，但轧空燃料烧完 + 费率转更负，别追高。回踩 1,780–1,800 才是轻多的位置，目标 1,860。

👉 SOL：最弱，4H 已转空排，多头被单边割 10:1 —— 想做多别选它。

⚠️ 方向别想当然：新闻显示这是【半关闭 + 谈判在加速】，不是全面封锁 → 油的跳空幅度大概率小于「海峡关闭」这个标题所暗示的。而且上一轮升级（7/9）黄金是【跌】的（油涨→通胀→加息重定价→压金价）。别指望「数字黄金」叙事这次会救 BTC。

📅 风险事件
· 今天 15:00（加州）—— CME Globex 重开，油/金/美股期货补定价三天地缘，本周最大跳空窗口
· 地缘尾部：「打谈交替」是固定节奏（打→谈→打→缓和），若周内传出「伊朗同意重开海峡」，追空的会被反向绞杀
· 周一 —— OPEC 月度原油报告｜美联储沃勒讲话｜伊拉克总理访华盛顿签油气协议
· 🔥 周二 7/14 05:30（加州）—— 美国 6 月 CPI：整体同比预期 3.8%（前值 4.2%）、环比 -0.10%、核心同比 2.9% 持平。口径提醒：6 月数据【不含】7 月这波油价，真杀伤在 8 月那份；但市场会提前交易预期
· 周三 —— 中国二季度 GDP（预期 4.5%）｜美国 6 月 PPI｜韩国央行（预期加息至 2.75%）｜周四 美国零售销售
· 财报潮：摩根系 / 高盛 / 奈飞 / 台积电｜Fed 7/28-29

STRAIT OF HORMUZ: THE GEOGRAPHY THAT CAN MOVE THE WORLD

The Strait of Hormuz is not merely a maritime passage; it is one of the clearest examples of how geography can be transformed into strategic power.

A narrow waterway between Iran and Oman has become the intersection point of global energy security, military competition and geopolitical influence.

Its importance comes from a simple reality: a significant share of the world’s energy flows through a corridor where disruption creates consequences far beyond the Middle East.

For Iran, Hormuz is a strategic equalizer — a geographic advantage that allows a regional power to impose costs on opponents with far greater conventional military strength.

Tehran does not need to defeat the U.S. Navy to achieve strategic effects; it only needs to create enough uncertainty to make global commerce slower, more expensive and more politically difficult.

Mines, missiles, drones, fast attack craft and electronic warfare are not designed only to destroy ships; they are designed to change behaviour.

The true weapon of a chokepoint is not only physical blockage, but uncertainty itself.

For the United States and its allies, protecting Hormuz is therefore not only about oil shipments; it is about preserving freedom of navigation and preventing strategic coercion from becoming an accepted international norm.

The contest is not simply over who can operate in the strait, but who can define the rules under which others operate.

Iran seeks to convert geography into leverage, demonstrating that proximity and regional position can compensate for conventional military imbalance.

Washington seeks to demonstrate that no single state can transform a global economic artery into a political bargaining instrument.

The economic consequences extend far beyond crude oil, affecting LNG, petrochemicals, fertilizer supply chains, insurance markets and inflation expectations.

Markets do not wait for complete closure; they react when the probability of disruption rises.

This is why Hormuz is so powerful strategically: perception can create real economic effects before a physical blockade exists.

The military struggle is therefore also a psychological and economic struggle.

Each side is attempting to convince the world that its preferred order is the only sustainable one.

Iran’s message is that regional geography gives it unavoidable influence.

The U.S. message is that international commerce cannot depend on the approval of a single regional actor.

The most likely outcome is not absolute victory for either side, but a new unstable balance where Iran retains disruption capability while the U.S. prevents permanent control.

The deeper lesson of Hormuz is that modern power is not measured only by fleets, missiles or economic size, but by the ability to influence global systems.

A few kilometres of water can therefore become a battlefield where energy markets, military strategy and international order collide.

The Strait of Hormuz remains one of the most important demonstrations that in the twenty-first century, geography itself can become a weapon.

"The Battle of the Guards": Trump Imposes Levies on Hormuz as Iran Threatens Comprehensive Regional War — By Talal Nahle

First: The Geopolitical Landscape and the Escalation of the American "Levy Doctrine"
The conflict over the Strait of Hormuz has transitioned from a phase of breaking the blockade to one of absolute sovereign dispute. In an unprecedented escalation, US President Donald Trump announced via the "Truth Social" platform that the United States is positioning itself as the "Guardian of the Strait of Hormuz," declaring the reimposition of a complete naval blockade on Iran. The most alarming aspect of Trump's announcement is the adoption of what can be termed the "Levy Doctrine." He confirmed the imposition of transit fees equivalent to 20% of the value of all shipments passing through the strait as compensation for protection costs.
This provocative stance was met with categorical Iranian rejection. The spokesperson for the Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters asserted that it is impossible to allow the United States to interfere in the management of the strait. In what constitutes the most severe warning since the war began, the Iranian leadership stated that any regional cooperation or American logistical support would be considered a "declaration of war against Iran's sovereignty," vowing that the flames of conflict would engulf all countries in the region. Iranian officials (such as Mohammad Mokhber and Ebrahim Rezaei) deemed the defense of Hormuz an existential decision to prevent the payment of protection money to foreign powers.

Second: Military and Field Assessment (The Technological Clash)
The battlefield witnessed a qualitative shift in offensive tactics, with the US Central Command (CENTCOM) announcing the first-time use of "suicide unmanned surface vessels" (USVs) in combat operations. Russia’s Oil Output Falls to Lowest in at Least 2 1/2 Years

Russia’s crude output declined to the lowest in at least two and a half years in June as Ukraine attacked the nation’s oil infrastructure on an almost daily basis.

Producers pumped an average of 8.928 million barrels a day of crude, according to a monthly report by the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries.

The June figure is 834,000 barrels a day below Russia’s required level for the month under an agreement with OPEC and its allies, and 61,000 barrels a day below May’s level, which was revised slightly lower, OPEC data based on secondary sources showed. (Bloomberg)
